articles.title: Economically active youths down in May amid job market uncertainties
articles.description: The labor force participation rate among Koreans aged 15 to 29 fell in May from a year earlier, data showed Thursday, reflecting growing difficulties in finding jobs amid economic uncertainties. The rate of economically active people in the age group stood at 47.2 percent in May, down 2.3 percentage points from a year earlier, according to the Ministry of Data and Statistics. The figure includes those who are employed as well as those actively seeking work. The unemployment rate for the age grou

articles.title: Military warns against N. Korean land mines washed away near inter-Korean rivers
articles.description: The South Korean military on Thursday called for caution against land mines planted by the North being washed into the South amid recent heavy downpours. The Joint Chiefs of Staff (JCS) issued the call, saying it found signs that some of the land mines planted en masse along the heavily guarded Demilitarized Zone (DMZ) separating the two Koreas were washed away by the recent downpours in the North. "There is a high probability that the land mines lost in water could be washed into the South via

articles.title: Supreme Court finalizes suspended prison term for ex-prosecutor for bribing ex-first lady
articles.description: The Supreme Court on Thursday confirmed a suspended prison term for a former prosecutor for bribing former first lady Kim Keon Hee in exchange for her help in his bid to run in an election. The top court upheld an appellate ruling that convicted Kim Sang-min on charges that included gifting a 140 million-won ($95,400) painting by renowned artist Lee Ufan to the wife of former President Yoon Suk Yeol in 2023. The former prosecutor was indicted on charges of violating the anti-graft law for giving
